extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT Wout won solo after surviving twice up Mont Ventoux, dropped peloton on first ascent, extended lead on descent to Malaucène finish.
tactical_patterns: ALAPHILIPPE attacked early Cat-4 climb to establish breakaway despite being world champion, revealing aggressive teams seed breaks early.
VAN AERT dropped GC contenders on first Ventoux ascent then extended advantage on technical descent, reversing typical climbing-descending dynamics.
VINGEGAARD briefly gapped POGACAR near summit second Ventoux ascension, first crack shown by yellow jersey against Danish climber.
ELISSONDE/BAKELANTS Trek teammates cooperated chase for second place, showing GC teams willing sacrifice break control for stage placings.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: Belgian champion jersey win on double-Ventoux queen stage proves classics specialists can survive HC climbs when motivated
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: only rider to momentarily gap Pogacar on final Ventoux ascent, signaling climbing parity with defending champion
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: negative: first visible trouble chasing Vingegaard at Ventoux summit before recovering, shows vulnerability at altitude after long efforts
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: world champion seeding breakaway on Cat-4 then fading reveals tactical role prioritized over personal result
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: negative: second place GC dropped 3m56s in single stage, reveals vulnerability on repeated HC climbs
